channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Container Registry Unavailability
item.description: May 27, 21:30 UTC Resolved - Customers might have seen intermittent unavailability with the container managed registry

item.title: Codex Context Compaction Latency
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe're investigating an issue causing Codex context compaction to be slower than expected.Affected components Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded performance)

item.title: 🔬ESMFold2: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub
item.description: Datasets vs. inductive bias, world models, and programmable biology

item.title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks — by Artificial Analysis and IBM
Android ChatGPT Business users having trouble switching workspaces
item.description: Status: InvestigatingAndroid ChatGPT users may have trouble switching workspaces. We are investigating and will provide updates as more information becomes available.Affected components Login (Degraded performance)

item.title: Elevated Latency and Errors on API
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe are still implementing the mitigation.Affected components Chat Completions (Degraded performance) Audio (Degraded performance) Embeddings (Degraded performance)

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: When an employee installs an AI writing assistant, connects a coding copilot to their IDE, or starts summarizing meetings with a new browser tool, they are doing exactly what a productive employee should do: finding faster ways to work. Across most organizations today, employees are running three...

item.title: Incident with Pull Requests, Issues, Git Operations and API Requests
item.description: May 27, 13:16 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. May 27, 12:54 UTC Update - We're continuing to investigate degraded performance of Git...

item.title: The Download: keeping up with AI, and the future of IVF
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Stay on top of what’s going on in AI this summer Here at MIT Technology Review, we understand exactly how relentless the pace of news from the world...

item.title: Designing secure access with ZTNA
item.description: New guidance explains how to design Zero Trust Network Access architectures aligned with zero trust principles and not built on old trust assumptions.

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Takedown Disrupts Developer Supply Chain Attack Infrastructure
item.description: CrowdStrike, in partnership with Google and the Shadowserver Foundation, has announced the simultaneous disruption of all command-and-control (C2) channels associated with GlassWorm, a persistent software chain campaign targeting software developers through malicious packages and extensions. "Since...

item.title: 3 SOC Steps that Shut Down Incident Risks Early
item.description: Most organizations still picture cyber defense as a fortress problem: build stronger walls, add more guards, buy another detection engine. But modern incidents rarely crash through the front gate. They drift in disguised as routine activity, hide inside legitimate processes, and quietly accumulate...

item.title: Romanian Hacker Sentenced to Prison in US for Selling Access to State Network
item.description: Catalin Dragomir previously pleaded guilty to selling access to an Oregon state government office’s network. The post Romanian Hacker Sentenced to Prison in US for Selling Access to State Network appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cloudflare Analytics availability degraded
item.description: May 27, 11:36 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. May 27, 11:14 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare Analytics is degraded and customers might experience elevated rate of errors, and or increased latency while reading data via Cloudflare API.

item.title: Lastwall Raises $11.5 Million for Quantum-Resilient Identity Platform
item.description: The new funding, led by BDC Capital’s StrongNorth Fund, will accelerate Lastwall’s North American expansion. The post Lastwall Raises $11.5 Million for Quantum-Resilient Identity Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Credential Crisis: How Stolen Credentials Defeat Modern Security
item.description: As AI accelerates phishing, session hijacking, and credential abuse, security teams are racing to close the gap between attacker speed and defensive response. The post The Credential Crisis: How Stolen Credentials Defeat Modern Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ‘SymJack’ Attack Turns AI Coding Agents Into Supply Chain Attack Delivery Systems
item.description: Malicious repositories and disguised symlinks can trick AI coding agents into silently installing attacker-controlled MCP servers capable of stealing secrets, compromising CI pipelines, and deploying malicious code. The post ‘SymJack’ Attack Turns AI Coding Agents Into Supply Chain Attack Delivery...

item.title: GlassWorm Botnet Disrupted
item.description: Security firms took down all four command-and-control (C&C) channels used by the GlassWorm malware. The post GlassWorm Botnet Disrupted appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Gitea Vulnerability Exposes Private Container Images without Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security flaw in Gitea, an open-source, self-hosted platform for version control, that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to pull private container images from Gitea deployments without requiring an account, password, or other credentials. The...

item.title: GigaDevice Explains Why EtherCAT is Essential for Robot Joint Control
item.description: GigaDevice recently hosted a webseminar titled "Secure Robotics in Motion," presenting the evolving direction of robot control systems. The company explained wh...

item.title: BYD Confirms Humanoid Robot Push with Seventh-Generation 'Yao-Shun-Yu' Project Targeting 4S Stores and Homes
item.description: BYD confirms its seventh-generation humanoid robot project targets global 4S dealership deployments, leveraging automotive manufacturing expertise to bridge the gap between China's hardware strength and AI intelligence.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 27, 09:41 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 27, 09:11 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 27, 08:04 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: XREAL Launches Affordable xbx Sub-Brand AR Glasses at 1,699 Yuan, Aims for IPO
item.description: XREAL launches its xbx sub-brand with 62g, 1,600-nit AR glasses priced at 1,699 yuan—the company's most affordable model to date—signaling a mass-market push ahead of an IPO.

item.title: Ant Group's Alipay Reaches 300M AI-Powered Transactions, Launches World's First AI-Native Payment Infrastructure
item.description: On May 26, Alipay announced that its AI-powered payment system has surpassed 300 million transactions, positioning itself as the world's first large-scale comme...

item.title: Huawei's Collaborative Authentication Rolls Out via Smart Glasses and Wearables
item.description: Huawei is gradually rolling out its collaborative authentication feature, which uses nearby connected devices such as earbuds and smart glasses to verify user i...

item.title: AI Chatbot Recommendations Redirect Users to Cryptojacking Malware Sites
item.description: Microsoft has warned of an active cryptojacking campaign that makes use of artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot interactions as a mechanism for surfacing malicious download sites. "This emerging delivery technique extends social engineering beyond conventional search results and increases the...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 27, 06:40 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 27, 06:01 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 27, 05:43 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization
item.description: arXiv:2605.26182v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating physically buildable brick structures from 3D shapes requires more than geometric reconstruction: the output must also satisfy discrete part constraints and structural stability. Existing brick generation methods either rely on heuristic...

item.title: Can LLMs Introspect? A Reality Check
item.description: arXiv:2605.26242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can large language models detect and report their own internal states? A number of studies have argued that the answer to this question is yes. We argue, based on lessons from human metacognition research, that this conclusion may be premature: to be...

item.title: Is Agent Memory a Database? Rethinking Data Foundations for Long-Term AI Agent Memory
item.description: arXiv:2605.26252v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-running AI agents need persistent memory. Memory supports learning across sessions, reduces repeated context injection, and enables auditing of past decisions. Current agent memory systems and database paradigms treat memory as storage. They...

item.title: Personalizing Embodied Multimodal Large Language Model Agents over Long-term User Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2605.26256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal large language model (MLLM)-based embodied agents have shown strong potential for solving complex tasks in physical environments. However, personalized assistance requires more than following generic instruction or recognizing object...

item.title: Constraint acquisition needs better benchmarks
item.description: arXiv:2605.26279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Constraint Acquisition (CA) and related research on the validation and enhancement of Mathematical Programming (MP) models from domain knowledge artifacts are currently limited by inadequate benchmarks. This deficiency impedes reproducibility and...

item.title: GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation
item.description: arXiv:2605.26121v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM pre-training efficacy increasingly depends on data composition rather than sheer volume. Yet, optimal mixing is hindered by categorization flaws: human taxonomies suffer from ontological misalignment, and Euclidean clustering fails to address...

item.title: The Constraint Tax: Measuring Validity-Correctness Tradeoffs in Structured Outputs for Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.26128v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Production LLM systems increasingly require machine-readable outputs: JSON objects, typed traces, regex-constrained fields, and tool-call schemas. This paper targets on-device and low-cost small language model (SLM) deployments, where sub-3B models...

item.title: AirCast-SR: A Foundation Model for Kilometer-Scale Atmospheric Super-Resolution via Latent Consistency Diffusion
item.description: arXiv:2605.26130v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Operational weather prediction at kilometer scales remains computationally prohibitive for traditional numerical weather prediction (NWP) models, limiting forecast access for applications in energy, agriculture, and disaster management that require...

item.title: SilIF: Silhouette-Augmented Isolation Forest for Unsupervised Transaction Fraud Detection
item.description: arXiv:2605.26135v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Unsupervised anomaly detection is widely used in transaction fraud detection where labels are scarce. Isolation Forest (IF) is among the most popular classical methods due to its scalability and ease of deployment. We propose SilIF, an augmentation of...

item.title: Neural Bayesian Sequential Routing
item.description: arXiv:2605.26147v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human decision-making is sequential and uncertainty-aware, yet standard neural networks often rely on static, dense forward computation with limited visibility into evidence acquisition, uncertainty evolution, or when computation should stop. We...

item.title: Self-Verified Distillation: Your Language Model Is Secretly Its Own Synthetic Data Pipeline
item.description: arXiv:2605.26132v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Can post-trained large language models (LLMs) further improve themselves using only unlabeled prompts, without external teachers or feedback from tools? We study this setting starting only from unlabeled seed questions with no ground-truth solutions,...

item.title: Pretraining Data Exposure in Large Language Models: A Survey of Membership Inference, Data Contamination, and Security Implications
item.description: arXiv:2605.26133v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become the predominant paradigm in NLP, advancing both research and industry. As model sizes and pretraining data grow, concerns about Pretraining Data Exposure (PDE) increase due to the scale and opacity of training...

item.title: SPEAR: Code-Augmented Agentic Prompt Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.26275v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automatic prompt engineering (APE) rewrites prompts to improve downstream task performance, but existing APE loops treat the optimizer itself as a fixed pipeline. We port the code-as-action paradigm of CodeAct (Wang et al., 2024a) to APE and propose...

item.title: CroCo: Cross-Lingual Contrastive Preference Tuning on Self-Generations
item.description: arXiv:2605.26293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work establishes that controlled contrastiveness between self-generated responses from large language models, set via reward scores, improves downstream preference tuning in English. We extend this method to multiple languages and evaluate two...

item.title: The Daily Dose: Workflow-Integrated Large Language Model Automation for Clinical Summarization and Trial Identification in Radiation Oncology
item.description: arXiv:2605.26346v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Objective: To describe the design and early clinical evaluation of The Daily Dose (TDD), an LLM-driven, automated clinical summarization and clinical-trial identification system integrated into routine radiation oncology practice. Design:...

item.title: [AINews] New AI Infra decacorns: Fireworks, Baseten (with OpenRouter on the way)
item.description: it's funding news, but it's good news.

item.title: Reachy Mini goes fully local
Shipping a Trillion Parameters With a Hub Bucket: Delta Weight Sync in TRL
Users unable to access chat support on help center
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Login (Operational)

item.title: AI Gateway Prompts not scanned for DLP
item.description: May 26, 19:52 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 26, 19:31 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues about not correctly scanning certain AI gateway prompts for DLP

item.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
item.description: Vision-language models (VLMs) commonly formulate visual grounding and detection as a coordinate-token generation problem, serializing each 2D box into multiple 1D tokens that are learned and decoded largely independently. This token-by-token decoding mismatches the coupled structure of box geometry...

item.title: When Eyes Betray AI: Social Gaze Consistency as a Semantic Cue for AI-Generated Image Detection
item.description: Recent generative models have largely closed the gap on low-level artifacts - pixel fingerprints, frequency anomalies, upsampling traces - particularly in person-centric and partial-edit settings where the manipulated region is small and surrounded by photometrically authentic content. We introduce...

item.title: Greening AI Inference with Accuracy and Latency-aware User Incentives
item.description: The widespread use of AI services has raised concerns for its environmental sustainability, towards which recent studies have identified carbon emissions of AI inference as the major contributor. This paper introduces a framework for designing AI inference incentives based on the users' valuation...
